item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Geograph
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: House for sale - Eden Townland
Author: Mac McCarron
Release year: 2008

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show edit
- Hide

To link to other content featuring Psi-7, I propose the following edit:

MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement" has been set up to contain individual occurrences of homes or people affected by SCP-1684.

to

MTF-ψ-7 "Home Improvement" has been set up to contain individual occurrences of homes or people affected by SCP-1684.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a usage of "amnestics" and a usage of "amnesiacs". Which one is intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this Euclid? It self-replicates, it's not confined or effectively tracked, and just for good measure, it eats people. (Sure, some of them have been found afterward, but in the meantime they've dropped off the face of the earth as far as their friends and neighbors are concerned, and the ones that have been located are permanently delusional.) This is textbook Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Meh." :/

The only saving grace is the excellent story attached to it. Still -1 though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I knew it! This is the cops again, isn't it? We are running a legitimate business, and you can't prove anything!

That's when I canceled my already-tenuous upvote. Other than that, my feelings about this are basically the same as wishun's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't like this. Whatever it is it's not MC&D, who always treat their customers with courtesy whatever evil things they ask for. This treats ordinary customers horribly. And running an estate agency isn't their style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like it, but I don't; and it's very frustrating that I can't pin down why I don't.

The basic idea is solid, an anomalous and crooked realty company. I like that the people and their stuff are found, vanishing-without-a-trace is an SCP trope and I like to see something different. Victims being immune to amnesiacs doesn't add much, but it another departure from standard. Every detail I focus on seems good, but as a whole I'm left with a generally negative reaction and nothing to blame it on. Very annoying.

No vote until I can figure out what my heads doing and have something constructive to say (don't hold your breath).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Homes found to be infected with SCP-1684 are to be occupied by task force members.

So MTF members are to act as permanent squatters, without the neighbors ever noticing and calling the police? It would probably be better to either have the property condemned or seized through imminent domain, to prevent anyone from buying it.

Foundation agents who have followed up on the advertised addresses have only found abandoned buildings, and phone numbers have led to inactive numbers.

I think you should make it a bit clearer that it's the Hearth Home Realty offices which appear abandoned.

After this disappearance, a sign advertising for Hearth Home Realty will appear at both the previously affected house and the house previously occupied by the disappeared persons.

So, X is living at 123 Main Street, Anytown USA. X sells the house to Y, buys an instance of SCP-1684 and moves into it. After X disappears and 123 Main Street becomes a new SCP instance, what happens to Y? Does he and his furniture disappear too? Does Y end up at the warehouses as well?

Also, what about the neighbors. Do they not notice that their neighbors keep disappearing?

These warehouses were found to be filled with furniture and other consumer goods believed to have originated from homes affected by SCP-1684. In addition, approximately 600 people who had previously disappeared due to SCP-1684's effect were located. When questioned, they reported that they believed they were still living in their home, and described their life and current circumstance as ideal.

What about the people who were observing the house when the occupants disappear? If they're present at the warehouse, do they think they're living at a non-existent house that they bought from Hearth Home Realty, or do they somehow integrate into the household that disappeared?

Affected persons did not respond to amnesiacs and were sent to mental institutions with forged identification documents.

Since the warehouses and their inhabitants escaped the notice of the locals before being found by the Foundation, why not let them continue living there while posting a guard around the warehouses? If they're sent to mental institutions they might contact relatives, and claims of being forced into a mental institution by a shadowy organization could be picked up on by various Groups of Interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of problems with the writing in this — missing or wrong words, subject-verb agreement, and so forth — and I don't think the idea is all that interesting, really. Or at least, the execution doesn't bring out its full potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was fun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda get MC&D vibes from this. Aside from that I'm not particularly taken by the idea, and the interview log is pretty much expected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *whispers* maybe a tale about it. And maybe they treat it like a private community, food just appearing somewhat magically. Of better, they steal it from towns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm….now I want to make something going into greater depth with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did they get food? Did no one notice them? Were they just considered crazy hobos? Did they react to each other?

You now have me curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish this went into the people living in a warehouse but still believing they were living a perfect life some more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I meant to say that in that quote from the article he refers to a house as SCP-1684, which it is not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This definitely needs a good spelling/grammar check, but it's kind of odd and quirky, and I like the fact that the apparently anomalous company are reacting in a completely non-anomalous (but thoroughly shady) way!

I'd agree though, SCP-1684 should be the company itself, SCP-1684-1 should be the affected house, and SCP-1684-2/A/whatever should be the affected persons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They can't believe you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this. One question, what happens if they're told of the effect AFTER moving in/visiting the office?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing about amnesiacs is nice. It means this effect is deeply planted in the mind of effected people. Which works, in this case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. There are a couple of things that stand out, though. First, I understand that you're using a Greek character to represent the MTF name, but the articles I've read explicitly translate the unit name; hence, MTF Psi-8. Also, the twenty seven seconds required to manifest the effect seems like an arbitrary number - I understand that it is, in actuality, an arbitrary number, but still, it seems artificial. Also, it seems to me that if the effect takes 6 to 8 weeks to "disappear" a subject, and afterwards also infects the house the subject previously lived in, there's a good chance that new occupants have already moved into that property. Are they also affected?

I appreciate that amnesiacs are not effective in affected subjects. You don't see that very often, it lends some weight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They think the company as selling homes that they want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"will have a sign will appear in front of the home advertising the Hearth Home Realty corporation"

I would just take out one of the "will"'s myself, but the tone feels wrong and I don't want to mess with it too much since I'm pretty new.

"Persons under this effect will view SCP-1684 as having "

SCP-1684's the mysterious realty company, isn't it? The houses are just affected by it, unless I misunderstood the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Documentation relating to the ownership of these homes appears to return to the company, as no deed has been found on the property or in any records the disappeared persons.

At this point Agent Gillespie was heard apparently heard by 'Wehrner' while she was giving questions to D-0145.

Oh Roget, it is your trademark.
